name: bat
description: A cat clone with syntax highlighting and Git integration. It provides syntax highlighting for a large number of programming and markup languages, shows git modifications, and integrates with other tools by design.

globalOptions.name: --language
globalOptions.description: Explicitly set the language for syntax highlighting. The language can be specified as a name or file extension (e.g. 'py', 'python', 'rs')

globalOptions.name: --list-languages
globalOptions.description: Display a list of supported languages for syntax highlighting

globalOptions.name: --theme
globalOptions.description: Set the theme for syntax highlighting. Use --list-themes to see all available themes. Use 'bat --list-themes | fzf --preview="bat --theme={} --color=always file"' to preview

globalOptions.name: --list-themes
globalOptions.description: Display a list of supported themes for syntax highlighting

globalOptions.name: --style
globalOptions.description: Configure which elements to display with the output. Comma-separated list of components: full, auto, plain, changes, header, header-filename, header-filesize, grid, rule, numbers, snip

globalOptions.name: --color
globalOptions.description: Control when to use colored output. Values: auto (default), never, always

globalOptions.name: --italic-text
globalOptions.description: Enable or disable the use of italic text in the output. Values: always, never (default)

globalOptions.name: --decorations
globalOptions.description: Control when to show decorations (line numbers, grid borders, header). Values: auto (default), never, always

globalOptions.name: --force-colorization
globalOptions.description: Force colorization even when piping output to another process. Alias for --decorations=always --color=always

globalOptions.name: --paging
globalOptions.description: Control when to use the pager. Values: auto (default), never, always. Auto pages when output is larger than one screen

globalOptions.name: --pager
globalOptions.description: Determine which pager to use. The default pager is 'less'. To control when the pager is used, use --paging

globalOptions.name: --map-syntax
globalOptions.description: Map a glob pattern to an existing syntax name. Usage: --map-syntax '*.build:Python' or --map-syntax 'Dockerfile*:Docker'

globalOptions.name: --show-all
globalOptions.description: Show non-printable characters like space, tab, and newline. Use --nonprintable-notation to control the notation

globalOptions.name: --plain
globalOptions.description: Only show plain style with no decorations. Equivalent to --style=plain. Use -pp to also disable paging (equivalent to -p --paging=never)

globalOptions.name: --number
globalOptions.description: Only show line numbers while suppressing the grid and header. Equivalent to --style=numbers

globalOptions.name: --diff
globalOptions.description: Only show lines that have been added/removed/modified with respect to the Git index. Use --diff-context to control the number of context lines

globalOptions.name: --diff-context
globalOptions.description: Include N lines of context around added/removed/modified lines when using --diff. Default: 2

globalOptions.name: --tabs
globalOptions.description: Set the tab width in spaces. Use 0 to pass tabs through directly. Default: 4

globalOptions.name: --wrap
globalOptions.description: Specify the text-wrapping mode. Values: auto (default), never, character

globalOptions.name: --terminal-width
globalOptions.description: Explicitly set the width of the terminal instead of detecting it automatically. Can use +N and -N for relative values

globalOptions.name: --line-range
globalOptions.description: Only print the specified range of lines for each file. e.g. '30:40' (lines 30-40), ':40' (first 40 lines), '40:' (from line 40)

globalOptions.name: --highlight-line
globalOptions.description: Highlight the specified line ranges. Can be specified multiple times. e.g. --highlight-line 40 or --highlight-line 30:40

globalOptions.name: --file-name
globalOptions.description: Specify the name to display for a file. Useful when piping data to bat from STDIN and you want a meaningful header

globalOptions.name: --strip-ansi
globalOptions.description: Specify when to strip ANSI escape sequences from the input. Values: auto (default), always, never

globalOptions.name: --nonprintable-notation
globalOptions.description: Set the notation for non-printable characters when using --show-all. Values: unicode (default), caret

globalOptions.name: --squeeze-blank
globalOptions.description: Squeeze consecutive empty lines into a single empty line

globalOptions.name: --squeeze-limit
globalOptions.description: Set the maximum number of consecutive empty lines to be printed when using --squeeze-blank. Default: 1

detectors.name: bat_config_dir
detectors.description: Detect bat configuration directory for custom themes and syntaxes
